title: The One Where They're Gonna PARTY!

lines.text: Outside Central Perk, Monica and Phoebe are showing everyone the van they bought for the catering business. It's an old Dodge van, that has a cartoon woman riding on a dragon painted on the side of it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on, no peeking! (They are leading the gang out with there hands over their eyes.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Our eyes are closed and we're about to cross the street. Very good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okayyyyy, open up!
They open their eyes and are stunned at the van.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What did you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitchin' van!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, it's for our catering business!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think I know that girl.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, umm, we're not gonna really keep it this way though.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, we're gonna paint over the sword, and replace it with a baguette.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And also, we don't know what to do with this. (She turns on a switch and the girl's nipples light up.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, I definitely know her.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, the gang is all there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Remember that guy from cooking school I told you about that put cilantro with everything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sure, Cilantro Larry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I'm gonna fill in for him as food critic for the Chelsea Reporter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, Monica! What an amazing opportunity to influence... dozens of people.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How could you say yes, what about our catering business?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, it's only one night a week, and plus I get to take all of you out for a lot of free dinners.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yay!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, in that case-(hops up and down in joy)-Yay! (Monica looks confused) That was me hopping on board.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys! Hey, Ross, quick question for ya. Are you ready to party?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, I could maybe go out for a couple of beers, but there's this thing about bumblebees on The Discovery Channel that I was planning to watch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no, I don't think you heard me. Are you ready to party?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nooo!! Gandolf?! Gandolf is coming to town?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Kathy's with her parents, I have nothing to do, so tomorrow we are partying with Gandolf dude!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, we are sooo gonna party!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Okay, dude alert! And who is this guy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mike "Gandolf" Ganderson, only like the funest guy in the world.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gonna call and get off work tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna call after you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is gonna be soo cool, dude, we never party anymore!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Woooo!!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, were you guys smoking something in the back of our van?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really. And what do you mean you never have fun anymore? You have fun with me, remember that time we saw those strippers and you paid me 50 bucks to eat that book?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, you are gonna love this guy. Gandolf is like the party wizard!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, why do you call him Gandolf?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gandolf the wizard. (Joey is still confused) Hello! Didn't you read Lord of the Rings in high school?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I had sex in high school.
Rachel's office, Rachel and Sophie are sitting at their desks working as Joanna walks in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, uh, Joanna I was wondering if I could ask you something. There's an opening for an assistant buyer in Junior Miss...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (interrupting) Okay, but that would actually be a big step down for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, actually, I meant for me. The hiring committee is meeting people all day and...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh. Well, I wish I could say no, but you can't stay my assistant forever. Neither can you Sophie, but for different reasons.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God, I am so glad you don't have a problem with this, because if you did, I wouldn't even consider applying.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Really? Well, in that case...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting) And that's I'm so glad... there's no problem.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: That's fine, actually I'm on the hiring committee, so there'll be at least one friendly face.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! That's great!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: You know, Junior Miss is where I started. Oh, I had to sleep with the ugliest guy to get that job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: No-ho-ho! (pause) Yeah. (pause) I mean, no-no-no-no-no, don't you worry, I'm sure with your qualifications you won't need to sleep with some guy to get that job. Although, I might need some convincing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I, umm...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Kidding! God, I feel wild today!
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is getting ready to party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh man! I am so excited-I may vomit!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you calm down, he's just a human guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look you don't understand, Gandolf is amazing. Y'know you're never know what's gonna end up happening, you go out for a couple of beers and end up on a fishing boat to Nova Scotia!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, it's beautiful country up there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Okay! I got my passport, fresh socks, and a snake bite kit!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not gonna be exactly like last time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'll see you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-wh-wh-whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have an audition, but I'll definitely hook up with you later. Where are you gonna be around noon?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Somewhere maybe along the equator?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (leaves as the phone rings)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (answering it) Hello. (listens) (to Ross) It's Gandolf!!! (on phone) So, are you in town? (listens) (disappointed) Oh, well, well maybe next time then. (Hangs up)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's not gonna make it, he's stuck in Chicago.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, man! Chicago, is sooo lucky!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stupid, useless Canadian money!
Bloomingdale's, Rachel is meeting with Mr. Posner, Mrs. Lynch, and Joanna the hiring committee.

lines.speaker: Mr. Posner
lines.text: You have a very impressive resume, Ms. Green. I especially like what I see here about implementing a new filing system.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Filing system? Oh-oh! You mean those-those little colored labels you put on all the folders? (to the committee) It certainly did brighten up the inside of the filing cabinets.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, they uh, they-they do more than that.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: I notice that you've been trusted with a lot of rather important responsibilities.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, Joanna really has been an incredible mentor to me.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh. And Rachel has been really incredible in getting my morning bagel for me. It's amazing how she gets it right almost every time!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I-I of course, I have more responsibilities than that.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh yes, well there's the coffee too. (to the committee) Rachel can carry two things at once!

lines.speaker: Mr. Posner
lines.text: Yes, that's very good. Now a uh, big part of this job is cultivating personal relationships, especially with designers.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I realize that...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (interrupting) And Rachel shouldn't have any problem with that. The only problem might be getting a little too friendly, if you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I love working with designers!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: With them, under them, what's the difference? Eh, Rach?
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is there as Monica enters carrying a huge stack of newspapers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! My first review is out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! Oh, the Chelsea Reporter, ohh, this used to keep me so warm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, look at my on the back page.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay! (reading) "Would I go back to Allesandro's? Sure, but I'd have to order two meals, one for me and one for the guy pointing the gun to my head." (to Monica) Wow! You really laid into this place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, they don't pay me a penny a word to make friends.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, I gotta go. I found a guy that who could fix up the van for catering.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Do you need me to go with you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, it's okay. But are we sure we don't want the waterbed?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Haven't we made this decision?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, all right. (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye!
The intercom buzzes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (answering it) Who is it?

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: It's Allesandro, from Allesandro's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: I want to talk to you about your review.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God. (on intercom) Call me on the phone!

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: Why? So you could hang up on me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, I-I'm never gonna let you up so you may as well just go away.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: Just give me a chance too...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (on intercom) Hey, do you need to get in? Here you go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Monica!
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: (entering) I want a retraction! Our food is not inedible swill!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I couldn't eat it! I had five friends who couldn't eat it, and one of them eats books.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: Well our service is not grossly incompetent.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The waiter carried the breadsticks in his pants!

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: Well, you said that we except the Discover Card, which we do not!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, that I'll retract. But I stand by my review, I know food and that wasn't it. You're marinara sauce tasted like tomato juice! You should serve it with vodka and a piece of celery.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: Hey! I'm proud of that sauce, it's delicious.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! You own an Italian restaurant and you think that tastes good?! Where are you even from?

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: (shyly) Lebanon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hand me those tomatoes, I'm gonna show you what it should taste like! Come on, hand me them.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: How long is this gonna take? 'Cause I got another critic to go yell at.
Rachel's office, Rachel is confronting Joanna about her interview.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering Joanna's office) Umm, Joanna? I wanna talk about that interview.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: I thought it went very well.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! It didn't! That's what I want to talk to you about. (starts to break up) Now, just to brief you... (starts to cry) I may cry, but they are not tears of sadness or of anger, but just of me having this discussion with you.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Rachel, please, don't make a scene.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There's nobody here!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Sophie, get in here! (Sophie enters) You see! Now you're making Sophie uncomfortable!

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: She's not making me uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Congratulations! You now just crossed the line into completely useless. Get out. (Sophie starts to cry and leaves)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you want me to quit?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: What?! What would make you think that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well of those things that you said in the interview, I mean if you believe any of them, I must not be a very good assistant. Y'know what? I am just gonna pack up my desk, (She goes over to get all of her belongings from the desk, which amount to a muffin and a pen) and I will be gone by the end of the day! (Realizes she has nothing.) Well, I guess there's no use to me sticking around 'til the end of the day! (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait-wait! You can put your sad little muffin back in it's drawer. If you must know the truth, I didn't want to lose a perfectly good assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: That's why I said all those things about your flirting and your drinking...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My drinking?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh, I must've said that after you left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Said what? Exactly.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: That you enjoyed the occasional drink...ing binge.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!! Ohh, that is it! I'm leaving! You are just a horrible person!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait-wait-wait-wait-wait-wait!! If you're gonna get all sensitive about it! I don't want to lose you. What if I, create a position for you? I'll make you an assistant buyer in this department.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Say more things like that.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: You can have your own office, and a raise! Effective tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'd need an expense account.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Done!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And an assistant.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Sophie, get in here! (Sophie peeks in around the corner)
Chandler and Joey's, Ross and Chandler are watching TV as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! What are you guys doing here? I thought you'd be out partying with Gandel-worf.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's Gandolf, and he's not coming.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you've been sittin' around here all mornin'?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! I balanced my checkbook.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, and I-I gave first names to all of the foosball players.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe he didn't come!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what if he didn't come! We can still go out and party ourselves!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-no, y'know with Gandolf we'd be out all night!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! We'd meet, we'd meet total strangers, and hang out with them!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we could do that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There's other stuff too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We'll do it all, and better! Look, after tonight, Gandolf will want to party with us, dude! Come on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not like we don't know how to party!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! All right? Let's go!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And may-maybe we could end up on a boat again?

lines.speaker: Joey and Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Chandler) Hey-hey-hey, when uh, when were we on a boat?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Remember that really cold morning, you woke up and those dogs were licking your face?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, those were seals, man.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is entering, excited.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Mon, little question for ya! How do you think this suit will look on an assistant buyer?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, the owner of Allesandro's came over to yell at me, but instead I made him some sauce, and he offered me the job as head chef!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!! You just ruined the thing I was practicing the whole way home, but I'm soo happy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you believe it? I finally get to run my own kitchen!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, you've waited soo long.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs, quick question for ya.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How do you think this suit would look on an assistant buyer at Bloomingdale's?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, it would totally depend on her coloring and... (realizes) You got the job!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You got the job?! Why didn't you tell me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, it's gonna be so great! I'm gonna get to help decide what we sell, I'm gonna have an office with walls and everything. (turns to Monica) I'm gonna have walls!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, is this the day of good news or what? I got us a job! The wedding reception.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh! Umm, Phoebe, I kinda need to talk to you about that. (Rachel excuses herself) Umm, well I-I-I think it might be time for me to take a step back from catering.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But we've only had one job.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, but now we have this second one and it just, it feels like it's snowballing, y'know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I got offered the head chef job at Allesandro's.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay, 'cause y'know what? You don't really need me for the business.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're the cook! With out you it's just me driving up to people's houses with empty trays and asking for money!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. But umm, I-I-I'll pay you back all the money you invested, and you can keep the van.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For what? I can't believe this! I gotta get out of here. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, wait a minute! (runs after her, leaving Rachel alone)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm an assistant buyer!!
Central Perk, Joey, Ross, and Chandler are making a pit stop on their party tour.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, so we'll get a little coffee, and get energized, and we'll head back out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, all right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, we're having fun, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We don't need that wizard guy. We hit a couple of clubs, talked to some strangers, and uh, after this, we'll head down to the docks and see about that boat thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm kinda beat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually, me too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you serious?!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank God! I'm exhausted!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: So you guys want coffees?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but uh, I don't want to be up too late, so uh, I'll have a decaf.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually, can I get some hot water with a little lemon? I think I strained my voice screaming in there. Does it have to be so loud?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't hear a word you're saying, my ears are ringing so bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm just glad I brought that extra pair of socks, y'know? I used them as mittens, I didn't want to touch a thing in that last place.
pause

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How sad are we?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? We're not sad, we're not sad, we're just not 21 anymore. Y'know? I'm 29 years old, damnit! And I want to sit in a comfortable chair, and watch television and go to bed at a reasonable hour!

lines.speaker: Joey and Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! And I like to hang out in a quiet place where I can talk to my friends.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And so what if I like to go home, throw on some Kenny G, and take a bath!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We're 29, we're not women.
Central Perk, the next day, Phoebe is there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Ohh, here you are. Y'know, I'm-I'm glad you decided to hear me out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm hearing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I've been doing a lot of thinking. A lot! And umm, well, I came up with a whole bunch of businesses you can do with your van. Okay umm, you could be flower delivery person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or! A bakery delivery person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I wa-I wa-I wa...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pizza?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I've got a whole bunch of uh-uh, stuff in this area, but umm, I'm getting the feeling that you don't want to deliver.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I'm guessing that if you don't want to deliver, you probably don't want to pick stuff up either.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, let's do the catering business.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, y'know I-I made a commitment to you. Y'know what, it'd be, it'd be fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! It will be fun! Ohh! Yay! Oh! Okay, ooh, let's plan the wedding reception. (She grabs the notebook which Monica used for her ideas and starts flipping page after page after page after page after page to find a blank one.) Wow! You really wanted me to do something with this van. (pause) Y'know what, I want you to take the chef job.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. That's what you really want. Yeah, I don't want to be the reason you're unhappy, that would just make me unhappy, and I really don't want to be the reason I'm unhappy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Besides, it might be kinda fun to form the new A-Team.
Rachel's office, she is coming in for the day carrying a picture for her new office. Mrs. Lynch is coming out of Joanna's office, carrying a box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi Mrs. Lynch! Is Joanna in already?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Oh my goodness! You haven't heard!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Heard what?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Joanna passed away last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! How?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Well, she was leaving work and she was hit by a cab.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh, I cannot believe it!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God. Oh, God. (gets worried) Oh God.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: I didn't realize that she was so close.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, so close. Mrs. Lynch, I know that this is an emotional and difficult time, for all of us. But by any chance did Joanna send any paperwork your way before... it happened.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: No. Nothing. Imagine, if she had just stepped off that curb a few seconds later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes-yes, just a few seconds and she'd still be with us-nothing about an assistant buyer?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: (starting to cry) No, I'm sorry. I have to go. (She leaves as Sophie arrives.)

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: (happily) Good morning!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Sophie, I guess you didn't hear about Joanna...

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: I sure did! (smiles)
Closing Credits
Monica's new kitchen, Allesandro is introducing her to her new employees.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: I'm so excited about having Monica come on board with us. Although I do feel bad about having fired chef Emillio, it's like losing a member of the family. Of course, that literally is the case for several of you. Tony, Carlos, Marie, please, tell your father how much we're gonna miss him. Now, I know that Monica has a lot of great ideas for this place, well, you all read the review. So without much further ado, I present to you our new head chef.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, I just wanna say, uh (reads from a 3 X 5 card) that with a pinch of exictement, a dash of hard work, a dollup of cooperation, we can have the recipe... (Looks up and sees eveyone glaring at her) Are you gonna kill me?
End
